text: Apple's long-awaited Siri overhaul has hit another snag during internal testing, pushing the launch beyond the original March target.
The revamped assistant will be powered by Google's Gemini model, marking a major shift from Apple's in-house approach.
New features include on-screen awareness, letting Siri understand what you're viewing and act on it instantly.
However, key capabilities like tapping into personal data and chaining multiple app actions have been delayed until later this year.
Bloomberg reports some features may not arrive until iOS twenty-seven in September, raising questions about Apple's AI timeline.
Apple is betting big on Google's Gemini to finally make Siri competitive.
But repeated delays show that integrating advanced AI into a privacy-focused ecosystem is far harder than building a chatbot from scratch.
